titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/281

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=261 status=live modified=2017-03-30 12:43:05.532608+00 | translation moa=260 status=live modified=2017-03-30 12:43:08.221762+00

p.cl: Vniuersis sancte Matris ecclesie filiis . Thomas de Lundin salutem .

Sciant presentes ⁊ futuri me dedisse ⁊
concessisse ⁊ Hac carta me confirmasse deo ⁊ ecclesie beate Marie de Nortberwic ⁊ Moniablibus ibidem
deo seruientibus in liberam ⁊ puram ⁊ perpetuam elemosinam duodecim Acras terre In Aquilon
ali parte de Adherenin . preterea dedi ⁊ concessi eidem Monialibus communem pasturam de terra mea ad ...[damaged]
cterum qui descendit ad capud de enoc derenin ex parte occidentali . Et ut capiant focale ad usus
suos ⁊ quod opus eis fuerit Infra easdem diuisas . Dedi Insuper ⁊ conccessi ⁊ quietam clamaui de me
⁊ Heredibus meis in perpetuum terram illam albam que fuit In contentatione Inter pratras ⁊ Math’riue .
⁊ uersus orientem sicut magna uia cadit In uallem . Tenendas sibi ⁊ successoribus suis de me
⁊ heredibus meis In liberam ⁊ puram ⁊ perpetuam elemosinam adeo libere ⁊ quiete plenarie ⁊ hon
orifice sicut aliqua elemosina de aliquo barone in regno scot’ liberius quietius plenius ⁊ honorificen
tius tenetur ⁊ possidetur .

Testibus Comite Malcolmo de fif . Nesso de Ramesia . Alexandro uuiet
Thomas de Kenmaron . Willelmo de Wiuilla . Alexandro de blalre . Alexandro de Sireis . Ricardo Anglico .
Waltero . ⁊ Alexandro capellanis . Hugoni clerico . DauiD de Atune . ⁊ Multis Aliis .
To all sons of Holy Mother Church, Thomas of Lundin sends Greeting:

Let present and future know that I have given and granted and by this my charter made firm to God and to the church of Blessed Mary at North Berwick and to the monks serving God there, in free and pure and perpetual alms, twelve acres of land on the north side of Aithernie. Futhermore, I have given and granted to the same nuns the common pasture of my land at ... which descends to the head of the ‘Cnoc derenin’ on the west side; and that they may take fuel for their own use and what shall be of use to them within the same bounds. I have given, moreover, and granted and quitclaimed for myself and my heirs in perpetuity the blanch land that was in dispute, between Pratis and Montrave, and towards the east as the high road falls into the valley. For them and their successors to hold of me and my heirs in free and pure and perpetual alms, as freely and quietly, fully and honourably, as any alms is freely, quietly, fully and honourably held and possessed of any baron in the kingdom of the Scots.

As witnesses, Earl Malcolm of Fife, Ness of Ramsay, Alexander Uviet, Thomas of Kilmaron, William of Wyville, Alexander of Blair, Alexander of Ceres, Richard English, Walter and Alexander, chaplains, Hugh the clerk, David of Ayton, and many others.
